Title: CRA Form NL428 and Worksheet NL428 2025 Newfoundland and Labrador resident tax.

Source:

- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5001-c/5001-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5001-d/5001-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5001-d/5001-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form NL428 2025 calculates Newfoundland and Labrador tax for Newfoundland and Labrador residents. Part A calculates Newfoundland and Labrador tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $44,192, $88,382, $157,792, $220,910, $282,214, $564,429, and $1,128,858. The rates are 8.7%, 14.5%, 15.8%, 17.8%, 19.8%, 20.8%, 21.3%, and 21.8%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $3,844.70, $10,252.25, $21,219.03, $32,454.04, $44,592.23, $103,292.95, and $223,516.33.

Part B calculates Newfoundland and Labrador non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $11,067. Worksheet NL428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $7,064, starts reducing above $38,712, is zero at $85,806 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $9,948 and a maximum of $9,043 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $7,467 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,410 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 8.7%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 8.7% and line 14 by 21.8%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Newfoundland and Labrador non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Newfoundland and Labrador tax. Line 65 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet NL428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 3.2% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 6.3%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 60%. Lines 69 and 70 subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover from tax and floor negative amounts at zero. Line 71 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 14 by 60%. Line 74 subtracts provincial foreign tax credit. Line 76 calculates the Newfoundland and Labrador political contribution tax credit: contributions over $1,150 receive $500; otherwise the first $100 receives 75%, the next range to $550 receives 50% plus $75, and amounts over $550 receive 33.33% plus $300. Lines 77 to 85 subtract political contribution credit, direct equity tax credit, resort property investment tax credit, current venture capital tax credit, and unused venture capital tax credit. Lines 86 to 108 calculate the low-income tax reduction. The basic reduction is $997, spouse and eligible dependant reductions are $557 each, the subtotal is capped at $1,554, adjusted family income thresholds are $40,460 with spouse or dependant reduction and $23,928 otherwise, and excess adjusted family income reduces the amount at 16%. Line 105 enters Newfoundland and Labrador tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from NL428 and Worksheet NL428 include 44192, 88382, 157792, 220910, 282214, 564429, 1128858, 0.087, 0.145, 0.158, 0.178, 0.198, 0.208, 0.213, 0.218, 3844.70, 10252.25, 21219.03, 32454.04, 44592.23, 103292.95, 223516.33, 11067, 7064, 38712, 85806, 0.15, 9948, 9043, 1000, 7467, 2410, 0.03, 0.60, 0.032, 0.063, 100, 550, 1150, 500, 0.75, 0.50, 0.3333, 75, 300, 997, 557, 1554, 40460, 23928, and 0.16.
